21.name: Shield Rush Tree Second Aoe Damage And Delay
21.description: Shield Rush's final impact echo deals significantly more hit damage, but the delay between the final impacts is longer.

22.name: Shield Rush Tree Second Aoe Crit Chance
22.description: Improves the base critical strike chance of Shield Rush's final impact echo.

23.name: Shield Rush Tree Second Aoe Crit Multi
23.description: Critical strikes from Shield Rush's final impact echo deal more damage.

4.name: Shield Rush Tree Forwards Beam
4.description: When you cast Shield Rush you create a {Void Beam} that travels with you, which is a spell that deals void damage over time in a small area. Shield Rush costs more mana.

6.name: Shield Rush Tree No Shield Requirement
6.description: Shield Rush no longer requires a shield and hits deal additional base void damage, but Shield Rush costs more mana.

12.name: Shield Rush Tree Return To Start
12.description:
When Shield Rush ends, you return to the position you used it from.

This removes Shield Rush's {Traversal} tag.

20.name: Shield Rush Tree Second Aoe
20.description: Shield Rush's final impact strikes a second time after a 0.5s delay, but Shield Rush hits deal less damage.

2.name: Hammer Throw Tree Attack Speed
2.description: Hammer Throw has increased attack speed.

3.name: Hammer Throw Tree Damage vs Proj Speed
3.description: Hammer Throw deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers), but hammers travels slower.

4.name: Hammer Throw Tree Conversion
4.description:
Hammer Throw can no longer spiral, chain, or orbit.

If your hammers would spiral, they instead deal significantly more hit damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).

If your hammers would chain, they instead deal more critical damage for each time they would chain.

If your hammers would orbit you, they instead have additional base critical strike chance.

Hammer Throw hits deal increased damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).

5.name: Hammer Throw Tree Free When Out of Mana
5.description: When you are out of mana Hammer Throw is free.

6.name: Hammer Throw Tree Shreds Armour
6.description: Hammer Throw hits have a chance to reduce enemy armor.

8.name: Hammer Throw Tree More Aoe Damage
8.description: {Disintegrating Aura} deals more damage, and Hammer Throw costs less mana.

9.name: Hammer Throw Tree Stun Chance
9.description: Hammer Throw deals more damage, and has a higher chance to stun enemies.

10.name: Hammer Throw Tree Stun Chance vs Pierce
10.description: Hammer Throw deals more damage, but Hammer Throw hits no longer pierce (travel through enemies hit). Its hits have a higher chance to stun enemies, and the stuns they inflict last longer.

12.name: Hammer Throw Tree Additional Chain
12.description: Hammers chain to additional enemies.

13.name: Hammer Throw Tree Damage To Stunned
13.description: Hammer Throw deals more hit damage against stunned enemies (multiplicative with other modifiers) and the stuns they inflict last longer

14.name: Hammer Throw Tree Health On Hit
14.description: Hammer Throw has a chance to grant you health on hit.

15.name: Hammer Throw Tree Mana On Hit
15.description: Hammer Throw rips a {Dark Orb} from enemies on hit, which travels back to you and grants 6 health and mana. Hammer Throw has a 5 second cooldown.

16.name: Hammer Throw Tree Projectile Speed
16.description: Hammers travel faster.

17.name: Hammer Throw Tree No Return
17.description: Hammer Throw hits have a chance to deal double damage, but hammers no longer return to you.

22.name: Hammer Throw Tree Mana Cost And Damage
22.description: Hammer Throw deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) and costs less mana.

23.name: Hammer Throw Tree Double Damage Chance
23.description: Hammer Throw hits have a higher chance to deal double damage.

24.name: Hammer Throw Stacking Buff
24.description: Using Hammer Throw grants you a stack of {Zeal} for 4 seconds, which increases your damage and critical strike chance by 10% per stack.

25.name: Hammer Throw Stacking Ignite Buff
25.description: Each stack of {Zeal} also grants you added global ignite chance.

7.name: Hammer Throw Tree Void Damage In Aoe
7.description: Your hammers gain a {Disintegrating Aura}, causing enemies nearby them to take void damage over time, but Hammer Throw costs additional mana and has less attack speed (multiplicative with other modifiers).

11.name: Hammer Throw Tree Stun Chain
11.description: Hammers chain to nearby enemies instead of returning to you. Hammer Throw can no longer throw extra projectiles, but it gains more damage and more attack speed (both multiplicative with other modifiers) for each extra projectile you would have thrown.

18.name: Hammer Throw Tree Extra Projectiles
18.description:
Hammer Throw launches additional hammers, but Hammer Throw costs more mana.

Hammers from the same throw cannot hit the same enemy, unless they spiral.

19.name: Hammer Throw Tree Nova Projectiles
19.description: Hammers are now thrown in a nova, with double the amount of additional hammers unless your hammers spiral. Hammer Throw hits deal additional throwing physical damage.

20.name: Hammer Throw Tree Spirals
20.description: Hammers now travel in a spiral around you, but halves your amount of additional hammers and Hammer Throw costs more mana.

21.name: Hammer Throw Tree Moves With Caster
21.description: Your spiralling hammers now orbit you, but Hammer Throw costs more mana.

3.name: Vengeance Damage While Prepped
3.description: You deal more global damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) while preparing to riposte.

5.name: Vengeance Attack Speed
5.description: Vengeance has increased attack speed.

6.name: Vengeance More Damage If Blocked Or Parried
6.description:
Vengeance deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).

This effect is doubled if you have blocked or parried a hit recently.

9.name: Vengeance Iron Blade Double Damage Chance
9.description: {Iron Blade} deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).

10.name: Vengeance Iron Blade Armour Shred Chance
10.description: {Iron Blade} has a chance to reduce enemy armor.

11.name: Vengeance Iron Blade Speed
11.description:
{Iron Blade} travels faster, causing it to also travel further.

Additionally, Vengeance and Riposte hits have a chance to attack with {Iron Blade} per point of Attunement up to a maximum.

15.name: Vengeance Parry Chance Per Forged Weapon
15.description: You gain an additional chance to parry per {Forged Weapon} for 4 seconds after using Vengeance.

16.name: Vengeance Damage Against Low Health Enemies
16.description:
Vengeance hits deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).

This effect is doubled against enemies on low health (below 35% health).

17.name: Vengeance Damage And Frailty Chance
17.description: Vengeance deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers), and its hits have a chance to apply {Frailty} on enemies.

19.name: Vengeance Reduced Damage Taken
19.description: You take less damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) and have increased armor if you've hit an enemy with Vengeance in the past 2 seconds.

20.name: Vengeance Void Essence On Riposte Hit
20.description: Vengeance and {Riposte} hits have a chance to grant a {Void Essence}.

21.name: Vengeance Void Cleave Or Erasing Strike Buff
21.description: Your next {Void Cleave} or {Erasing Strike} deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) after you use and hit with Vengeance.

22.name: Vengeance Armour Debuff
22.description: Vengeance hits have a chance to shred enemy armor and physical resistance.

24.name: Vengeance Armour Shred Effect
24.description: Increases the effectiveness of all armor shred applied by Vengeance and {Riposte}.

26.name: Vengeance Bleed Effect From Phys Res
26.description: Vengeance gains additional physical penetration with bleed per 1% overcapped physical resistance.

28.name: Vengeance Riposte Fire Damage And Ignite Chance
28.description: Riposte deals more fire damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) and its hits have a chance to ignite enemies.

2.name: Vengeance Double Retaliation
2.description: You can {Riposte} an additional time after Vengeance hits an enemy, but Riposte mitigates less damage.

4.name: Vengeance Damage Additional Retaliations
4.description: You can {Riposte} an additional time after Vengeance hits an enemy.

7.name: Vengeance Iron Blade On Vengeance Hit
7.description: Vengeance and Riposte hits have a chance to attack with {Iron Blade}.

14.name: Vengeance Iron Blade Forged Weapon Chance
14.description: {Iron Blades} now have a chance to be summoned as {Forged Weapons} instead.

30.name: Vengeance Cannot Be Crit When Riposting
30.description:
Hits that you {Riposte} cannot critically strike you.

Additionally, improves Vengeance's base critical strike chance.

8.name: Vengeance Fire Conversion
8.description:
Vengeance' base physical damage is converted to fire. Consequently this damage scales with increases to fire damage, but not increases to physical damage or other damage types.

Chance to shred physical resistance from all sources is converted to chance to shred fire resistance.

Bleed chance from all sources is converted to ignite.

Swaps Vengeance' {Physical} tag for a {Fire} tag.

13.name: Vengeance Gladiator Arsenal
13.description: {Iron Blade} ricochets to additional enemies, but travels more slowly.

18.name: Vengeance Void Conversion
18.description:
Vengeance' base physical damage is converted to void. Consequently this damage scales with increases to void damage, but not increases to physical damage or other damage types.

Chance to shred physical resistance from all sources is converted to chance to shred void resistance.

Bleed chance from all sources is converted to chance to inflict {Time Rot} for Vengeance.

Swaps Vengeance' {Physical} tag for a {Void} tag.

23.name: Vengeance Bleed Chance
23.description: Vengeance hits have a chance to cause enemies to bleed.

32.name: Vengeance Time Rot And Echo Chance
32.description:
Vengeance hits have a chance to inflict {Time Rot}.

Additionally, Vengeance has additional chance to Echo if it can Echo.

33.name: Vengeance Undodgeable Echo
33.description:
Vengeance deals more void damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) and additional melee void damage.

Additionally, Echoed Vengeance cannot be dodged.

12.name: Vengeance Crit Multi
12.description: Vengeance critical strikes deal more damage.

34.name: Vengeance Frenzy And More Fire Damage Per Ignite
34.description:
Vengeance has a chance to grant you {Frenzy} for 2 seconds when hitting ignited enemies.

Additionally, Vengeance deals more fire hit damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) per ignite on the enemy, up to a maximum.

2.name: Erasing Strike Tree Void Rift AoE
2.description: Erasing Strike and {Void Rifts} it creates deals damage in a larger area.

3.name: Erasing Strike Tree Void Rift Time Rot
3.description: Erasing Strike hits have a chance to inflict enemies with {Time Rot}, an ailment that deals void damage over time, increases stun duration by 5%, lasts 3 seconds, and stacks up to 12 times.

5.name: Erasing Strike Tree Damage Over Time
5.description: Erasing Strike deals more damage over time.

6.name: Erasing Strike Tree Always Creates Void Rift
6.description: Erasing Strike always creates a {Void Rift} at its impact location.

7.name: Erasing Strike Tree Void Rift Increased Damage Taken
7.description: Erasing Strike hits shred enemy void resistance.

9.name: Erasing Strike Tree Damage Against Damaged
9.description: Erasing Strike has increased cooldown recovery speed and deals more melee damage to enemies that are already damaged (multiplicative with other modifiers).

10.name: Erasing Strike Tree Damage Against Full Health
10.description: Erasing Strike deals more melee damage to enemies at full health (multiplicative with other modifiers).

11.name: Erasing Strike Tree Damage And Cull
11.description: Erasing Strike deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) and instantly kill enemies that are below a health threshold (a percentage of their maximum health).

16.name: Erasing Strike Tree Damage And Mana Cost
16.description:
Erasing Strike deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers). 

This bonus is doubled while wielding two handed weapon.

17.name: Erasing Strike Tree Crit Chance
17.description:
Improves Erasing Strike's base critical strike chance.

Additionally, Erasing Strike's hits cannot be dodged.

19.name: Erasing Strike Tree Crit Multiplier
19.description: Erasing Strike critical strikes deal more damage.

21.name: Erasing Strike Tree Speed On Kill
21.description: You gain the {Haste} buff for 1 second, granting 30% increased movement speed when Erasing Strike kills an enemy or hits a boss or rare enemy.

23.name: Erasing Strike Tree Lower Beam Requirement
23.description: The kills required to summon {Void Beams} via the {Ravenous Void} node require less kills to trigger, but Erasing Strike costs more mana.

24.name: Erasing Strike Void Damage With Sword Axe Mace
24.description:
Erasing Strike deals more void damage if you are using a two handed mace (multiplicative with other modifiers).

Erasing Strike has increased cooldown recovery speed if you are using a two handed sword.

Erasing Strike's melee hit and void rifts have a larger area of effect if you are using a two handed axe.

8.name: Erasing Strike Tree Stationary Void Beam
8.description: When Erasing Strike kills an enemy you have a chance to cast {Void Beam} at their location, a spell that continuously damages surrounding enemies for 3 seconds.

18.name: Erasing Strike Tree Void Essence On Crit
18.description:
Erasing Strike critical strikes have a chance to grant you a {Void Essence}, which grant 15% increased void damage, 15% increased melee damage, and 10% reduced stun duration. 

You can have a maximum of 3 void essences at once.

20.name: Erasing Strike Tree Life On Kill
20.description: When Erasing Strike kills an enemy or hits a boss or rare enemy you regain health equal to a percentage of the target's maximum health.

22.name: Erasing Strike Tree Void Beams
22.description: If you kill 5 or more enemies simultaneously with Erasing Strike, you create 4 {Void Beams} which travel at random, damaging surrounding enemies for 3 seconds.

4.name: Erasing Strike Void Beam vs Boss
4.description: When Erasing Strike hits a boss or rare enemy you have a chance to cast {Void Beam} at their location, a spell that continuously damages surrounding enemies for 3 seconds.

12.name: Erasing Strike Void Beam Damage
12.description: All {Void Beams} created by Erasing Strike deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).

13.name: Erasing Strike Consumes Mana For Echoes
13.description: Erasing Strike consumes mana to cause additional echoes when used. Each echo will appear one second after the last. These additional echos cannot be created while you have negative mana.

14.name: Erasing Strike No Cooldown
14.description: Erasing Strike costs significantly less mana, but now has a cooldown.

15.name: Erasing Strike Slow Chance
15.description: Erasing Strike hits have a chance to slow and deals more melee damage to slowed enemies (multiplicative with other modifiers).

26.name: Erasing Strike Bleed to Time Rot Conversion
26.description:
Bleed chance from all sources is converted to {Time Rot} chance for Erasing Strike and effects related to bleed now depend on Time Rot instead. 

Erasing Strike deals more melee damage against targets with 12 stacks of Time Rot (multiplicative with other modifiers).

27.name: Erasing Strike Additional Charge
27.description: You can store an additional charge of Erasing Strike.

28.name: Erasing Strike Cooldown Recovery
28.description: Erasing Strike has increased cooldown recovery speed, but also has a higher mana cost.

3.name: Devouring Orb Tree Orbit Distance
3.description: Devouring Orb deals more damage and orbits you at a greater distance.

4.name: Devouring Orb Tree Autocast While Moving
4.description:
While you are moving, you will automatically cast Devouring Orb if it is not on cooldown and you have enough mana.

This effect puts Devouring Orb on cooldown.

6.name: Devouring Orb Tree Increased Damage
6.description:
Devouring Orb deals more hit damage to enemies it collides with (multiplicative with other modifiers).

This ONLY affects the damage that devouring orb deals when the orb itself collides with an enemy, not the damage dealt by its void rifts or other sub skills.

7.name: Devouring Orb Tree Damage During Duration
7.description:
You deal more global void damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) and have increased mana regen while a Devouring Orb is active.

These bonuses are doubled if you have at least 90 vitality.

8.name: Devouring Orb Tree Duration vs growth
8.description: Devouring Orb lasts longer, but a Devouring Orb's {Void Rifts} no longer increase in size or damage for each successive rift.

10.name: Devouring Orb Tree Abyssal Orb Frequency
10.description: The rate at which Devouring Orbs cast {Abyssal Orb} is increased by a random amount.

12.name: Devouring Orb Tree Reduced Duration
12.description: Devouring Orb costs less mana, but has a shorter duration (multiplicative with other duration modifiers).

13.name: Devouring Orb Tree Cast Speed
13.description: Devouring Orb has increased cast speed and a shorter cooldown.

14.name: Devouring Orb Tree Cooldown Recovery
14.description: Devouring Orb has a shorter cooldown and {Void Eruption} deals more damage

15.name: Devouring Orb Tree Rift Damage
15.description: All damage from Devouring Orb penetrates enemy void resistance. This includes {Void Rifts} and other sub skills and sources of damage added by this tree, and any ailments they inflict

16.name: Devouring Orb Tree Rift Damage Growth
16.description: The rate at which {Void Rift} hit damage and area increments when a nearby enemy dies is increased.

17.name: Devouring Orb Tree Rift AoE Growth
17.description: {Void Rift} hits deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) to {Time Rotting} enemies.

18.name: Devouring Orb Tree AoE
18.description: Devouring Orb deals damage in a larger area.

19.name: Devouring Orb Consume Buffs
19.description: When you directly cast non-orbiting Devouring Orb within the area of an existing Devouring Orb, the new Orb consumes all existing Orbs to deal more damage in a larger area (both multiplicative with other modifiers), per Orb consumed. However, the new Orb also deals less ailment damage per Orb consumed (multiplicative with other modifiers).

20.name: Devouring Orb Tree Rift Time Rot
20.description:
Devouring Orb lasts longer and it has a chance to inflict {Time Rot} on enemies each second inside its area of effect.

This chance also applies to hits from its subskills.

21.name: Devouring Orb Tree Rift Damage vs Time Rot
21.description: Devouring Orb has a chance to apply frailty to enemies within its area of effect each second.

22.name: Devouring Orb Tree More Radius Per Second
22.description: Devouring Orb gains more radius each second, up to a maximum.

23.name: Devouring Orb Tree More DoT Less Hit
23.description: Devouring Orb deals more damage over time, but less hit damage (both multiplicative with other modifiers).

24.name: Devouring Orb Tree Autocast Chance For Refund
24.description: Whenever {Abyssal Rush} triggers, it has a chance to consume only 50% of Devouring Orb's cooldown and mana cost.

25.name: Devouring Orb Tree Added Charges
25.description: Devouring orb gains additional charges, but costs more mana.

26.name: Devouring Orb Tree Protection During Duration
26.description: You have a chance to gain a {Void Essence} when Devouring Orb expires.

2.name: Devouring Orb Tree Orbits
2.description: Devouring Orb now orbits you, but has a much shorter duration (multiplicative with other duration modifiers).

5.name: Devouring Orb Tree Deals Damage
5.description: Devouring Orb now deals void damage to enemies it collides with.

9.name: Devouring Orb Tree Abyssal Orb
9.description: Every 3 seconds Devouring Orb releases an {Abyssal Orb}, a spell which seeks enemies and detonates, dealing void damage in an area.

11.name: Devouring Orb Tree Erupts On Death
11.description:
Devouring Orb now erupts at the end of its duration, creating a {Void Eruption}.

Devouring Orb has a much shorter duration (multiplicative with other duration modifiers).

28.name: Devouring Orb Tree Abyssal Orb Slow And Shred
28.description: Devouring Orb hits have a chance to slow and shred enemy void resistance.

2.name: Abyssal Echoes More Damage
2.description: Abyssal Echoes deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).

3.name: Abyssal Echoes Armor Shred Stacks
3.description: Abyssal Echoes' initial burst shreds enemy armor.

4.name: Abyssal Echoes Armor Shred Effect
4.description: Armor shred applied by Abyssal Decay has increased effect.

5.name: Abyssal Echoes No Cooldown Added Mana Cost
5.description: Abyssal Echoes no longer has a cooldown, but costs more mana.

6.name: Abyssal Echoes Mana Efficiency
6.description: Abyssal Echoes costs less mana.

7.name: Abyssal Echoes Trigger Nearby Devouring Orbs
7.description: Directly using Abyssal Echoes has a chance to cast a {Void Rift} from each nearby {Devouring Orb}.

8.name: Abyssal Echoes Targeted Cast
8.description: Abyssal Echoes can now be cast at the target location.

9.name: Abyssal Echoes Increased Duration
9.description: Abyssal Decay has increased duration.

10.name: Abyssal Echoes Blinds
10.description: Abyssal Decay now blinds enemies.

11.name: Abyssal Echoes Reduce Physical Res But No Trigger On Hit
11.description: Abyssal Decay causes enemies to have reduced physical resistance, but it is no longer consumed when the enemy is hit.

12.name: Abyssal Echoes Increased DoT Taken
12.description: Abyssal Decay causes enemies to take increased damage over time.

13.name: Abyssal Echoes Damage When Hit Rather Than Over Time
13.description: Abyssal Decay now deals damage when the enemy is hit, rather than over time. Each hit deals a portion of the full damage Abyssal Decay would have dealt over its full duration. This damage still counts as damage over time.

14.name: Abyssal Echoes DoT And Melee Attack Speed Buff
14.description: Directly using Abyssal Echoes grants you {Nether Coating} for 5 seconds, granting you increased attack speed with your melee and throwing attacks and causing you to deal more damage to blinded enemies.

15.name: Abyssal Echoes Time Rot On Hit With Buff
15.description: {Nether Coating} also grants you a chance to inflict {Time Rot} on hit.

16.name: Abyssal Echoes Consume Bleeds For Instant Damage
16.description: Abyssal Echoes' initial burst consumes all stacks of bleed on the enemy, causing their damage to be dealt instantly.

17.name: Abyssal Echoes Slow Stacks
17.description: Abyssal Echoes' initial burst slows enemies.

18.name: Abyssal Echoes Slow To Haste Conversion From Enemies
18.description: Abyssal Decay causes Enemies that would apply {Slow} to instead grant their target {Haste} for 1 second.

19.name: Abyssal Echoes Next Erasing Strike Or Void Cleave More Melee Damage To Slowed
19.description: After casting Abyssal Echoes your next {Erasing Strike} and {Void Cleave} deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) to slowed enemies.

20.name: Abyssal Echoes Increased Area
20.description: Abyssal Echoes affects a larger area.

21.name: Abyssal Echoes Ignite Chance
21.description: Abyssal Echoes' initial burst has a chance to ignite enemies.

22.name: Abyssal Echoes Void Spell Damage On Hit No Decay
22.description: Abyssal Echoes' initial burst now hits and deals spell void damage, but no longer inflicts Abyssal Decay.

23.name: Abyssal Echoes Fire Conversion
23.description:
Spell void damage from {Potent Corruption}, {Vorpal Reverberation} and attribute scaling is converted to fire. Consequently this damage scales with increases to fire damage, but not increases to void damage.

Abyssal Echoes' initial burst now also heals you and your allies and nodes on this tree that grant more damage also grant more healing.

24.name: Abyssal Echoes Attunement Scaling
24.description: Abyssal Echoes' damage now scales with attunement rather than vitality, and its healing effectiveness is also increased by attunement.

25.name: Abyssal Echoes More Damage No Chain
25.description: Abyssal Echoes deals significantly more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers), but no longer chains.

26.name: Abyssal Echoes Ailment Spread
26.description: Abyssal Decay now spreads to several nearby enemies after 1 second.

27.name: Abyssal Echoes Ailment Spread Modification
27.description: Abyssal Decay spreads to enemies further away and can spread to more enemies when it spreads.

29.name: Abyssal Echoes Abyssal Rift
29.description: Abyssal Echoes now creates an {Abyssal Rift} at the target location that casts Abyssal Echoes for you 3 times at its location before expiring.

28.name: Abyssal Echoes Abyssal Rift Echoes More
28.description: {Abyssal Rifts} cast additional Abyssal Echoes.

31.name: Abyssal Echoes More Chains Tripled For Echoes
31.description:
Abyssal Echoes can chain additional times. 

This effect is tripled for echoed casts.

32.name: Abyssal Echoes Echoed Casts More Damage
32.description: Echoed casts deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).

5.nodeName: Summon Skeleton Ward On Skele Death
5.name: Summon Skeleton Ward On Skele Death

6.nodeName: Summon Skeleton Mana On Skele Death
6.name: Summon Skeleton Mana On Skele Death

7.nodeName: Summon Skeleton Spell Damage On Skele Death
7.name: Summon Skeleton Spell Damage On Skele Death

8.name: Summon Skeleton Increased Speed

21.nodeName: Summon Skeleton Bone Armor On Allies
21.name: Summon Skeleton Bone Armor On Allies

24.nodeName: Summon Skeleton Melee Range And Damage
24.name: Summon Skeleton Melee Range And Damage

3.nodeName: Summon Skeleton Heal Skeleton On Skeleton Death
3.name: Summon Skeleton Heal Skeleton On Skeleton Death

0.name: Root Node
0.description: Rips blood out of a target enemy dealing physical damage to it. A {Blood Orb} is drawn back to you which restores 10 health when it reaches you.

4.name: Rip Blood Tree Area
4.description: {Blood Splatter} deals damage in a larger area.

5.name: Rip Blood Tree Damage Over Time
5.description: Rip Blood deals more damage over time (multiplicative with other modifiers).

12.name: Rip Blood Tree Buff Minion Damage
12.description: {Blood Splatter} grants your minions {Blood Infusion} when it hits them. The {Blood Infusion} buff grants 15% more damage for 4 seconds (multiplicative with other modifiers).

13.name: Rip Blood Tree Area Per Minion
13.description: Blood Splatter has increased area for each minion you currently control, up to a maximum benefit.

14.name: Rip Blood Tree Buff Minion Attack Speed
14.description: {Blood Infusion} grants increased attack and cast speed.

15.name: Rip Blood Tree Buff Minion Leech
15.description: {Blood Infusion} causes your minions to leech a portion of the physical damage they deal as health.

16.name: Rip Blood Tree Cast Speed
16.description: Rip Blood has increased cast speed.

17.name: Rip Blood Tree Mana Cost And Cast Speed
17.description: Rip Blood costs less mana and has increased cast speed.

18.name: Rip Blood Tree Free When Out Of Mana
18.description: When you are out of mana Rip Blood is free.

19.name: Rip Blood Tree Added Health Gained
19.description: {Blood Orbs} grant additional health and the {Blood Orb} returns faster.

20.name: Rip Blood Tree More Health Per Intelligence
20.description: {Blood Orbs} restore more health based on your Intelligence stat.

21.name: Rip Blood Tree Mana Gained
21.description: {Blood Orbs} now also grant you mana when you directly cast Rip Blood. The amount of mana is tripled for orbs from bosses and rare enemies.

23.name: Rip Blood Tree Crit Chance
23.description: Rip Blood has additional critical strike chance..

24.name: Rip Blood Tree Marrow Shards
24.description: When you directly use Rip Blood and hit a boss or rare enemy or hit at least 3 enemies, you cast Marrow Shards in the same direction. Marrow Shards still consumes health when cast this way.

26.name: Rip Blood Tree Spell Damage On Hit
26.description: When you directly cast Rip Blood and hit at least one enemy you gain additional spell damage for 6 seconds. The amount of spell damage gained is doubled for Rip Blood. This effect can stack.

27.name: Rip Blood Tree Upfront Damage
27.description: Rip Blood deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).

28.name: Rip Blood Tree Upfront Damage Per Minion
28.description: Rip Blood deals more damage for each minion you currently control (multiplicative with other modifiers), up to a maximum benefit.

31.name: Rip Blood Tree Health To Ward
31.description:
{Blood Orbs} now grant ward instead of health, equal to half of the health they would have restored.

Being at or close to full health does not affect the amount of the ward the {Blood Orbs} grant.

2.name: Rip Blood Tree Splatter Chance
2.description: Rip Blood hits have a chance to create a {Blood Splatter} at the target, dealing damage to other enemies around it that were not hit by Rip Blood. This effect can occur a limited number of times per cast of Rip Blood.

3.name: Rip Blood Tree Blood Tether
3.description: When you directly cast Rip Blood and hit a boss or rare enemy it attaches a {Blood Tether}, dealing physical spell damage over time.

22.name: Rip Blood Tree Targets Minions
22.description: Rip Blood can be cast on your minions, but no longer can be cast on enemies. {Blood Orbs} restore significantly more health.

29.name: Rip Blood Tree Cast Again On Kill
29.description:
When you directly cast Rip Blood and it kills at least one enemy, it will recast from that position towards another enemy, consuming Rip Blood's mana cost. These recasts can also trigger this effect.

Rip Blood no longer creates {Blood Orbs}.

30.name: Rip Blood Tree Necrotic
30.description:
Rip Blood and {Blood Splatter's} base physical damage are converted to necrotic damage. Consequently this damage scales with increases to necrotic damage, but not increases to physical damage.

Bleed chance from all sources is converted to Damned chance.

32.name: Rip Blood Tree Target For Minions
32.description: Rip Blood has increased cast speed leaves a stench on the target enemy that causes your minions to prioritise attacking it.

6.name: Rip Blood Tree Coagulated Blood
6.description:
When a blood orb grants health, you gain a stack of {Coagulated Blood}. Upon reaching at least 6 stacks, your next cast of Rip Blood consumes 6 stacks to form a {Coagulated Blood Orb} that seeks out a nearby enemy, dealing spell physical damage in a small area.

Additionally, Blood Tethers such as from {Blood Bond} grant 3 stacks each second.

8.name: Rip Blood Tree Coagulated Blood Damage And Area
8.description: The {Coagulated Blood Orb} deals more damage in a larger area (multiplicative with other modifiers).

10.name: Rip Blood Tree Bleed Chance
10.description: Rip Blood hits have a chance to cause enemies to bleed..

2.name: Sacrifice Tree Damage Vs Mana Cost
2.description: Sacrifice deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers), but Sacrifice costs more mana.

3.name: Sacrifice Tree Damage If Minion Has More Health
3.description: Sacrifice deals significantly more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) if the sacrificed minion has more current health than you.

4.name: Sacrifice Tree Damage If Only One Minion
4.description: Sacrifice deals significantly more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) if the minion you sacrifice is your only active minion.

5.name: Sacrifice Tree Added Fire Damage
5.description: Sacrifice deals additional fire damage.

6.name: Sacrifice Tree Mana Efficiency and DoT on Cast
6.description: Sacrifice costs less mana and if you cast Sacrifice while you control at least one minion you gain a buff that grants you more damage over time (multiplicative with other modifiers). This buff does not stack.

7.name: Sacrifice Tree Ignite Chance
7.description: Sacrifice has a chance to ignite enemies and ignite it inflicts has additional fire penetration.

8.name: Sacrifice Tree Damage Vs Bleeding
8.description: Sacrifice deals more damage against bleeding enemies (multiplicative with other modifiers).

9.name: Sacrifice Tree Damage Per Minion
9.description: Sacrifice deals more damage for each minion you currently control (multiplicative with other modifiers), up to a maximum.

11.name: Sacrifice Tree Blood Wraith Bleed
11.description: {Blood Spectres} have a chance to cause enemies to bleed. Blood Spectres consume less mana when summoned.

12.name: Sacrifice Tree Blood Wraith Health And Damage
12.description: {Blood Spectres} summoned by Sacrifice have more maximum health and deal more damage (both multiplicative with other modifiers).

13.name: Sacrifice Tree Blood Wraith Size
13.description:
Sacrificing a minion always creates a {Blood Spectre}.

Blood Spectres summoned by Sacrifice have significantly more maximum health and damage (both multiplicative with other modifiers), and are larger, but consume more mana when summoned.

14.name: Sacrifice Tree Blood Wraith Leech
14.description: {Blood Spectres} leech a portion of the damage they deal as health. Sacrifice has a greater chance to summon a Blood Spectre.

16.name: Sacrifice Tree Bone Nova Speed
16.description: {Bone Nova} projectiles travel faster, causing them to travel further.

17.name: Sacrifice Tree Bone Nova Damage
17.description: {Bone Nova} deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).

18.name: Sacrifice Tree Bone Nova Pierces
18.description: {Bone Nova} projectiles pierce (continue through targets they hit).

19.name: Sacrifice Tree Bone Nova Bleed Chance
19.description: {Bone Nova} hits have a chance to cause enemies to bleed.

20.name: Sacrifice Tree DoT Damage On Cast
20.description: You deal increased damage over time if you have cast Sacrifice recently (past 4 seconds). You cannot have multiple instances of this buff at once.

21.name: Sacrifice Tree Area And Stun Chance
21.description: Sacrifice deals damage in a larger area and has an increased chance to stun enemies.

22.name: Sacrifice Tree Area With Three Minions
22.description: Sacrifice deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) in a larger area if you have three or more minions active when you cast Sacrifice.

10.name: Sacrifice Tree Blood Wraith
10.description:
When you sacrifice a minion you have a chance to assemble a {Blood Spectre} from the remains. 15 mana is consumed when you summon a Blood Spectre. 

You cannot summon a Blood Spectre by sacrificing a Blood Spectre or when your mana is negative and below -100.

15.name: Sacrifice Tree Bone Nova
15.description: Sacrificing a minion creates a {Bone Nova} around it, but Sacrifice costs more mana.

23.name: Sacrifice Tree Chains
23.description: Sacrifice is recast onto other minions within its area of effect, which can happen multiple times. Sacrifice costs more mana.

24.name: Sacrifice Tree Additional Chains
24.description: Sacrifice is recast onto other minions within its area of effect up to one additional time. It also has additional Bleed Chance.

25.name: Sacrifice Tree Infernal Shade
25.description: If you cast Sacrifice while you control at least one minion all your active {Infernal Shades} have their remaining duration reduced to 2 seconds, but deal significantly more damage for their remaining duration.

3.name: Marrow Shards Tree Physical Leech On Cast
3.description: You leech a portion of physical damage as health if you have cast Marrow Shards recently (past 4 seconds).

5.name: Marrow Shards Tree Splinter Damage
5.description: {Bone Splinters} deal more damage.

6.name: Marrow Shards Tree Splinter Speed
6.description: {Bone Splinters} travel faster and further.

7.name: Marrow Shards Tree Splinter Bleed Chance
7.description: {Bone Splinters} have a chance to cause enemies to bleed.

8.name: Marrow Shards Tree Splinter Damage Against Bleeding
8.description: {Bone Splinters} hits deal more damage against bleeding enemies (multiplicative with other modifiers).

9.name: Marrow Shards Tree Splinter Crit Chance
9.description: Improves {Bone Splinters'} base critical strike chance.

10.name: Marrow Shards Tree Splinter Crit Multiplier
10.description: {Bone Splinters} critical strikes deal more damage.

11.name: Marrow Shards Tree Damage Vs Range
11.description: Marrow Shards and {Bone Splinters} hits deal more damage, but Marrow Shards travels less far.

12.name: Marrow Shards Tree Crit Chance And Multiplier
12.description: Marrow Shards and {Bone Splinters} have improved base critical strike chance and critical strikes deal more damage.

13.name: Marrow Shards Tree Crit Multiplier
13.description: Marrow Shards and {Bone Splinters} deal more damage with critical strikes.

14.name: Marrow Shards Tree Health Lost On Crit
14.description: Marrow Shards and {Bone Splinters} critical strikes deal more damage, but you lose health when you critically strike with Marrow Shards.

17.name: Marrow Shards Tree Ends At Target
17.description: Marrow Shards are destroyed at the location you target if it is within their maximum range. Marrow Shards costs more health.

18.name: Marrow Shards Tree Double Cast Chance
18.description: When you directly cast Marrow Shards, you have a chance to immediately cast a second instance of Marrow Shards. The additional cast does cost health.

19.name: Marrow Shards Tree Armour Shred Chance
19.description: Marrow Shards and {Bone Splinters} hits have a chance to shred enemy armor.

21.name: Marrow Shards Tree Health On Minion Kill
21.description: You gain health when you kill one of your own minions.

22.name: Marrow Shards Tree Crit Chance On Kill
22.description: Grants you increased critical strike chance if you have killed with Marrow Shards or {Bone Splinters} recently (past 4 seconds).

23.name: Marrow Shards Tree Leech On Kill
23.description: You leech a portion of physical damage as life if you have killed with Marrow Shards recently (past 4 seconds).

24.name: Marrow Shards Tree Bleed Chance And Health Cost
24.description: Marrow Shards and {Bone Splinters} have a chance to cause enemies to bleed, but Marrow Shards costs more health.

26.name: Marrow Shards Tree Chance To Return Health
26.description: Marrow Shards has a chance to create a {Marrow Orb} on hit that returns 15 health to you.

27.name: Marrow Shards Tree Health Returned
27.description: {Marrow Orbs} return more health.

28.name: Marrow Shards Tree Additional Chance For Health Return
28.description: Marrow Shards hits have a higher chance to create a {Marrow Orb}.

29.name: Marrow Shards Tree Mana Returned
29.description: {Marrow Orbs} have a chance to also replenish mana.

30.name: Marrow Shards Tree Damage Against Bleeding
30.description: Marrow Shards hits deal more damage against bleeding enemies (multiplicative with other modifiers).

2.name: Marrow Shards Tree No Pierce
2.description: Marrow Shards and {Bone Splinters} hits deal significantly more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers), but Marrow Shards no longer pierces.

4.name: Marrow Shards Tree Splinters
4.description: Marrow Shards creates {Bone Splinters} in a cone behind the enemy when destroyed, damaging other enemies, but Marrow Shards no longer pierces and costs more health.

16.name: Marrow Shards Tree Splinters At End
16.description: Marrow Shards creates {Bone Splinters} in a cone when destroyed, which damage enemies, but Marrow Shards travels less far and costs more health.

20.name: Marrow Shards Tree Damages Minions
20.description:
Marrow Shards and {Bone Splinters} deal more damage, but Marrow Shards can hit and damage your own minions. 

On hit effects, such as health gained on hit, do not proc when you hit your minions.

25.name: Marrow Shards Tree Consumes Minion
25.description: Marrow Shards hits deal significantly more damage, but consumes one of your minions when you cast it.

32.name: Marrow Shards Cold Conversion
32.description:
Marrow shards's base physical damage is converted to cold damage. Consequently this damage scales with increases to cold damage, but not to physical damage. It also gains a base freeze rate.

Bleed chance from all sources is converted to frostbite chance.

Swaps Marrow Shards's physical tag for a cold tag.

2.name: Summon Wraith Tree Can Target
2.description: {Wraiths} appear at the target location, but Summon Wraith costs more mana.

3.name: Summon Wraith Tree Stationary
3.description: {Wraiths} deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) and have increased melee attack speed, but are unable to move.

4.name: Summon Wraith Tree Melee Attack Speed
4.description: {Wraiths} have more melee attack speed (multiplicative with other modifers).

6.name: Summon Wraith Tree Reduced Cooldown
6.description: Summon Wraith has a shorter cooldown, and there is a shorter delay between each {Wraith} being summoned.

7.name: Summon Wraith Tree Additional Wraith
7.description: Summon Wraith summons additional {Wraiths} per cast, but costs more mana.

9.name: Summon Wraith Tree Increased Health
9.description: {Wraiths} have more maximum health (multiplicative with other modifiers).

11.name: Summon Wraith Bleed Effect
11.description: Bleed inflicted by {Wraiths} has increased effect and duration.

12.name: Summon Wraith Size and AoE
12.description: {Wraiths} are larger, deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers), and hit a larger area with their melee attacks.

14.name: Summon Wraith Tree Added Necrotic Damage
14.description: {Wraiths} deal additional necrotic damage with attacks and spells.

15.name: Summon Wraith Tree Cast Speed and Mana Efficiency
15.description: Summon Wraith has increased cast speed and costs less mana.

16.name: Summon Wraith Tree Move Speed
16.description: {Wraiths} move faster.

18.name: Summon Wraith Tree Mana Crit
18.description: {Wraiths} have improved base critical strike chance, and when you cast Summon Wraith you gain increased critical strike chance equal to its mana cost for 4 seconds.

19.name: Summon Wraith Tree Wraith Crit Chance
19.description: {Wraiths} have additional critical strike chance and leech a portion of the damage they deal with crits as health

20.name: Summon Wraith Tree Wraith Crit Multiplier
20.description: {Wraiths'} critical strikes deal more damage.

21.name: Summon Wraith Tree Necrotic Leech
21.description: {Wraiths} deal more necrotic damage (multiplicative with other modifiers), and leech a portion of the necrotic damage they deal as health.

22.name: Summon Wraith Tree Tanky
22.description: {Wraiths} have more maximum health (multiplicative with other modifiers), but Summon Wraith costs more mana and has reduced cast speed.

23.name: Summon Wraith Tree Melee Poison Chance
23.description: {Wraiths}' have additional posion penetration and their melee attacks have a chance to poison enemies on hit.

25.name: Summon Wraith Tree Reduced Drain
25.description: {Wraiths}' health decays more slowly.

26.name: Summon Wraith Tree Health On Crit
26.description: Your critical strikes restore health to your {Wraiths}.

28.name: Summon Wraith Tree Bleed Chance
28.description: {Wraiths} have a chance to cause enemies to bleed on hit.

29.name: Summon Wraith Tree Damned Chance On Death
29.description: {Wraiths} have a chance to apply {Damned} to nearby enemies on death.

30.name: Summon Wraith Tree Increased Drain and Ward Gen
30.description: Gain ward for each {Wraith} summoned, but their health decays more quickly.

5.name: Summon Wraith Tree Multiple Wraiths
5.description: Summon Wraith summons additional {Wraiths} with each cast, but Summon Wraith also costs more mana and now has a cooldown.

8.name: Summon Wraith Tree Instant Wraiths
8.description: Removes the delay between each {Wraith} being summoned, but fewer Wraiths are summoned per cast (rounded up).

10.name: Summon Wraith Persistent Wraiths
10.description: {Wraiths'} health no longer decays, but you are limited to only 2 wraiths.

17.name: Summon Wraith Tree Flame Wraith Chance
17.description: You have a chance to instead summon a {Flame Wraith}, which casts fireballs. Additionally all {Wraiths} deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).

24.name: Summon Wraith Tree Putrid Wraith Chance
24.description: You have a chance to instead summon a {Putrid Wraith}, which Poisons enemies from a distance. Additionally all {Wraiths} deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).

13.name: Summon Wraith Max Wraiths
13.description: You can control more {Wraiths} at once.

2.name: Wandering Spirits Summon More
2.description: Wandering Spirits reveals Spirits more frequently.

3.name: Wandering Spirits Slower Spirits
3.description: Spirits deal more damage when they pass through enemies (multiplicative with other modifiers), but move slower.

4.name: Wandering Spirits Increased Radius
4.description: Spirits are revealed in a larger radius around you.

6.name: Wandering Spirits Reduced Radius
6.description: Spirits are revealed in a smaller radius around the player and deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).

7.name: Wandering Spirits Long Lasting
7.description: Spirits last longer when they are revealed.

8.name: Wandering Spirits Shreds Necrotic Res
8.description: Wandering Spirits shred the necrotic resistance of enemies near them.

10.name: Wandering Spirits Increased Fear Range
10.description: Spirits can Fear enemies in a larger radius. If spirits inflict {Necrotic Resistance Shred} or {Damned} those ailments are also inflicted in a larger radius.

11.name: Wandering Spirits Shorter Fear
11.description: Spirits deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers), but the fear they apply to enemies via the {Terrifying Presence} node has a shorter duration.

12.name: Wandering Spirits Longer Fear
12.description: Fear applied to enemies via the {Terrifying Presence} node lasts longer, but affects a smaller area.

13.name: Wandering Spirits More Damage While Feared
13.description: Spirits deal more damage to feared or possessed enemies (multiplicative with other modifiers).

14.name: Wandering Spirits Reduced Mana Cost
14.description: Wandering Spirits has increased cast speed and costs less mana.

15.name: Wandering Spirits More Spawning Duration
15.description: Wandering Spirits continues to reveal Spirits for a longer duration, but has a longer cooldown.

16.name: Wandering Spirits Increased Size
16.description:
Each Spirit is larger and deals damage in a larger area.

If you have {Terrifying Presence}, the area for inflicting fear is also increased.

17.name: Wandering Spirits Ward on Death
17.description: You gain Ward whenever a Spirit expires.

18.name: Wandering Spirits Mana on Death
18.description: You have a chance to gain mana whenever a Spirit expires.

19.name: Wandering Spirits Faster Spirits
19.description: Spirits move faster and deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).

20.name: Wandering Spirits More Damage to Poisoned
20.description: Spirits deal more damage to poisoned enemies (multiplicative with other modifiers).

22.name: Wandering Spirits Increased Spit Range
22.description: Spirits cast {Spectral Putrescence} at enemies from further away.

23.name: Wandering Spirits Increased Spit Rate
23.description: Spirits can cast {Spectral Putrescence} more frequently.

5.name: Wandering Spirits Poison Conversion
5.description: The base necrotic damage dealt by Wandering Spirits is converted to poison damage. Consequently this damage scales with increases to poison damage, but not increases to necrotic damage.

9.name: Wandering Spirits Fear
9.description: Enemies near Spirits are {Feared} for 0.5 to 0.9 seconds, causing them to flee from the spirit that feared them.

21.name: Wandering Spirits Poison Spit
21.description: Spirits have a chance to cast {Spectral Putrescence} at nearby enemies each second. Spectral Putrescence is a projectile with 100% chance to poison on hit.

24.name: Wandering Spirits At Target
24.description: Spirits are now revealed around a target location, rather than around you. Spirits deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).

26.name: Wandering Spirits More Damage to Cursed
26.description: Spirits deal more damage to cursed enemies and damned enemies (multiplicative with other modifiers).

28.name: Wandering Channelled
28.description: Wandering Spirits is now a channelled skill, has a much shorter cooldown, and creates Spirits much more frequently. You can regenerate mana while channelling this skill.

30.name: Wandering Spirits Reduced Cooldown
30.description: Wandering Spirits has a shorter cooldown

31.name: Wandering Spirits Applies Damned
31.description: Wandering Spirits inflict enemies around them with {Damned}.

2.name: Hungering Souls Reduced Projectiles
2.description: Souls deal additional spell damage based on your maximum mana, but fewer Souls are created with each cast.

4.name: Hungering Souls Increased Crit Chance
4.description: Improves Hungering Souls' base critical strike chance.

5.name: Hungering Souls Crit Multi
5.description: Souls' critical strikes deal more damage.

6.name: Hungering Souls Even Bigger
6.description: Souls are larger and deal more hit damage, but Hungering Souls costs more mana.

8.name: Hungering Souls More Mana On Kill
8.description: Souls grant you more mana when they kill an enemy.

9.name: Hungering Souls Reduced Spread
9.description: Hungering Souls fires projectiles in a narrower cone.

10.name: Hungering Souls Poison Chance
10.description: Souls have a chance to poison enemies.

11.name: Hungering Souls Subtracts From Mana Cost
11.description: Souls have an increased chance to poison enemies, and they cost less mana.

12.name: Hungering Souls DoT Damage on Cast
12.description: Directly casting Hungering Souls causes you to deal more damage over time against Possessed Enemies for 8 seconds. This effect does not stack.

13.name: Hungering Souls Extra Projectiles Chance
13.description: Hungering Souls has a chance to fire additional projectiles.

15.name: Hungering Souls Increased Spread
15.description: Hungering Souls costs less mana, and fires projectiles in a wider cone.

16.name: Hungering Souls Added Speed
16.description: Hungering Souls has increased cast speed and its souls travel faster, causing them to go further before they expire.

17.name: Hungering Souls Cull Chance
17.description:
Souls hits instantly kill enemies that are below a health threshold (a percentage of their maximum health). 

If you have a similar effect from an item or passive node then the highest threshold is used.

18.name: Hungering Souls Added Necrotic Damage
18.description: Souls deal additional necrotic damage on Hit.

21.name: Hungering Souls More Retaliation
21.description:
You have a higher chance to cast Hungering Souls when hit.

When you directly cast Hungering Souls you gain Ward.

22.name: Hungering Souls Reduced Damage and More Cast When Hit
22.description:
You have a higher chance to cast Hungering Souls when hit.

When you directly cast Hungering Souls you have a chance to cast it twice in quick succession.

23.name: Hungering Souls Damage With A Minion
23.description: Souls deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) if you have at least one minion.

24.name: Hungering Souls Damage Per Minion
24.description: Souls deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) for each minion you have.

25.name: Hungering Souls Damage Exactly 3 Minions
25.description: Souls deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) if you have exactly three minions.

20.name: Hungering Souls Ward On Kill
20.description: Hungering Souls cost less mana and has a chance to grant ward on kill.

28.name: Hungering Souls Ignite Power
28.description: Ignites inflicted by Hungering Souls last longer and have additional Fire Penetration

29.name: Hungering Souls More Damage Over Time
29.description: Souls deal more damage over time (multiplicative with other modifiers).

3.name: Hungering Souls Solo Soul
3.description: Souls are significantly larger and deal more hit damage, but only one is created with each cast.

7.name: Hungering Souls Mana On Kill Chance
7.description: Souls have a chance to grant you mana when they kill an enemy.

14.name: Hungering Souls Extra Projectiles
14.description: Hungering Souls fires additional projectiles, but costs more mana.

19.name: Hungering Souls Cast When Hit
19.description: You have a chance to cast Hungering Souls when hit, but Souls cast this way have a 70% chance to only have one projectile.

27.name: Hungering Souls Fire Conversion
27.description:
Hungering Souls' base damage is increased and converted to fire and its hits ignite instead of possessing. Consequently this damage scales with increases to fire damage, but not increases to necrotic damage.

Souls deal more hit damage.

12.name: Lunge More Damage Along Path
12.description: Lunge deals more damage to enemies along its path and with its final hit.

18.name: Lunge Added Crit Chance With Polearm
18.description: Improves Lunge's base critical strike chance when using a spear.

22.nodeName: Lunge Physical Penetration Buff Per Enemy Hit
22.name: Lunge Physical Penetration Buff Per Enemy Hit

4.name: Lunge Added Charges
4.description: Lunge gains an additional charge, but it has a longer cooldown.

9.name: Lunge Damage Along Path
9.description: Lunge now hits all enemies along its path to your target.

25.name: Lunge Invincible While Lunging
25.description: You are invulnerable while Lunging.

2.name: Lunge Recover Missing Health
2.description: You regain a percentage of your missing health when you use Lunge.

3.name: Lunge Increased Path Area
3.description: Lunge deals damage in a wider area along its path.

6.name: Lunge Allies In Path Gain Haste
6.description: Other allies caught in the path gain {Haste} for a short duration.

7.name: Lunge To Allies
7.description: You can now Lunge to other allies.

8.name: Lunge Heal Allies
8.description: You now heal allies and cleanse negative ailments on allies you Lunge to. The amount of health gained is increased by healing effectiveness.

11.name: Lunge Chance To Inflict Bleed Along Path
11.description: Lunge hits have a chance to cause enemies to bleed along its path but not its final hit.

14.name: Lunge Fire Conversion
14.description:
Lunge's base physical damage is converted to fire. Consequently this damage scales with increases to fire damage, but not increases to physical damage or other damage types.

Bleed chance from all sources is converted to ignite chance.

Swaps Lunge's {Physical} tag for a {Fire} tag.

This has no effect if you have taken the {Voidbringer} node.

15.name: Lunge Block Chance While Lunging
15.description: You have a high chance to block while Lunging.

16.name: Lunge Frailty Chance
16.description: Lunge's final hit has a chance to apply {Frailty} to enemies based on distance traveled up to a maximum.

17.name: Lunge Immobilize On Final Hit
17.description: Lunge's final hit now {Immobilizes} enemies for a short duration.

20.name: Lunge More Damage And Area Base On Distance
20.description: Lunge's final hit deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) in a larger area based on distance traveled up to a maximum.

21.name: Lunge Culling
21.description:
Lunge hits instantly kills enemies that are below a health threshold (a percentage of their maximum health). The threshold is based on distance traveled up to a maximum.

If you have a similar effect from an item or passive node then the highest threshold is used.

24.name: Lunge Smite Chance
24.description: Lunge's final hit has a chance to cast {Smite} at up to 3 nearby targets based on distance traveled up to a maximum.

27.name: Lunge Frenzy On Kill
27.description: Killing an enemy with Lunge grants you {Frenzy} for a short duration.

28.name: Lunge Void Conversion
28.description:
Lunge's base physical damage is converted to void. Consequently this damage scales with increases to void damage, but not increases to physical damage or other damage types.

Bleed chance from all sources is converted to chance to inflict {Time Rot}.

Swaps Lunge's {Physical} tag for a {Void} tag.

29.name: Lunge Kills Resets Big Melee Skill Cooldowns
29.description: Killing an enemy with Lunge resets the cooldowns of {Void Cleave}, {Erasing Strike}, {Forge Strike} and {Judgement}

30.name: Lunge Polearm Thrust
30.description: Lunge's final hit now deals damage in an extended rectangle instead of a circle when using a spear.

31.name: Lunge Use Javelin When Reaching Target With Polearm
31.description:
Lunge's final hit now causes you to use {Javelin} when using a spear.

This consumes Javelin's mana cost.

32.name: Lunge Reduce Cooldown With Other Attacks
32.description:
You reduce a portion of Lunge's remaining cooldown when you use a different melee attack and hit at least one enemy. 

This effect can only occur a limited number of times per use of Lunge.

33.name: Lunge Melee Damage To High Health Enemies
33.description: Lunge deals more melee damage to enemies at high health (multiplicative with other modifiers).

34.name: Lunge Haste To Nearby Allies Based On Distance
34.description: You grant other nearby allies {Haste} at the end of the charge for a short duration based on distance traveled, up to a maximum.

35.name: Lunge More Cooldown Reduction And Heals
35.description: {Warrior's Renewal} recovers an additional amount of Lunge's cooldown and also grants you health when it triggers.

2.name: Flame Reave Mana Efficiency Vs Speed
2.description: Flame Reave has increased attack speed and costs less mana, but travels more slowly, reducing its range.

3.name: Flame Reave Every 4 Attacks Large
3.description: Using Flame Reave grants 4 stacks of {Rhymth of Fire}. If you already have 12 stacks, Flame Reave instead consumes all the stacks to gain greatly increased width, range, and damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).

4.name: Flame Reave Surge And Mana Strike Count
4.description: Flame Reave travels faster.

5.name: Flame Reave Spreading Flames
5.description: Flame Reave hits deal more damage against ignited enemies (multiplicative with other modifiers).

6.name: Flame Reave Flat Fire Damage
6.description:
Flame Reave hits have a chance to grant you {Frenzy} for 3 seconds.

In addition to Frenzy's base effect it now grants Flame Reave additional melee fire damage.

7.name: Flame Reave Flat Fire With Swords
7.description: Enemies killed by Flame Reave have a chance to explode, dealing damage to nearby enemies, but Flame Reave costs more mana.

9.name: Flame Reave More Fire Damage
9.description: Flame Reave deals more fire damage (multiplicative with other modifiers)

11.name: Flame Reave Explosion Power
11.description: You have increased fire damage and fire protection if you have dealt a critical strike with Flame Reave recently (past 4 seconds).

12.name: Flame Reave More Melee Damage
12.description: Flame Reave deals more melee damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) and it deals added melee damage based on your added spell damage.

13.name: Flame Reave Melee Damage
13.description: Flame Reave has a chance to ignite, but a narrower area of effect.

16.name: Flame Reave Cooldown and Haste
16.description: Flame Reave hits deal more damage.

17.name: Flame Reave Cooldown Recovery
17.description: Flame Reave has a shorter cooldown.

19.name: Flame Reave Pull on Return
19.description: Flame Reave pulls enemies with it while it returns to you, but now has a cooldown. Does not increase the duration of the cooldown if Flame Reave already has one.

20.name: Flame Reave Crit Narrow
20.description: Flame Reave hits enemies in an expanding circle centered on you, but has less range and costs significantly more mana.

21.name: Flame Reave Ward on Crit
21.description: When you attack with Flame Reave and critically strike at least one enemy you gain ward.

22.name: Flame Reave Ignite Chance
22.description: Flame Reave hits have a chance to ignite enemies.

23.name: Flame Reave Damage vs Ignited
23.description: Flame Reave hits deal more melee damage against ignited enemies (multiplicative with other modifiers).

24.name: Flame Reave Crit vs Ignited
24.description: Flame Reave hits deal more melee fire damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).

25.name: Flame Reave Speed and Range
25.description: Flame Reave travels faster, increasing its range.

26.name: Flame Reave Crit Multi
26.description: Flame Reave's critical strikes deal more damage.

27.name: Flame Reave Increased Width
27.description: Flame Reave deals damage in a larger area.

14.name: Flame Reave Fireball Chance
14.description: Flame Reave critical strikes deal significantly more damage, but Flame Reave’s area of effect is always half as wide as the default.

8.name: Flame Reave Lightning Conversion
8.description:
Half of Flame Reave's base damage is converted to lightning, meaning that this half of the damage scales with increases to lightning damage but not with increases to fire damage.

Fire damage bonuses from this tree now also apply to Lightning.

Ignite chance from this tree now also grants an equal amount of shock chance.

Flame Reave gains a {Lightning} tag.

10.name: Flame Reave Explosion on Kill
10.description: Enemies killed by Flame Reave have a chance to explode, dealing damage to nearby enemies.

18.name: Flame Reave Returns
18.description: Killing an enemy with Flame Reave makes you harder to stun for 4 seconds.

28.name: Flame Reave Circle
28.description: Flame Reave hits enemies in an expanding circle centered on you, but has less range and costs significantly more mana.

29.name: Flame Reave Fire Aura
29.description:
You have a chance to cast {Fire Aura} when you use Flame Reave.

Flame Reave has additional chance to Ignite on hit per active Fire Aura.

30.name: Flame Reave Rhythm of Fire Crit
30.description:
Flame Reave requires fewer stacks of {Rhythm of Fire} to consume all stacks. This does not affect the bonuses from consuming stacks.

Additionally when Flame Reave consumes {Rhythm of Fire} stacks it gains additional critical strike chance.

2.name: Black Hole Damage and Less Pull
2.description: Black Hole deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers), but Black Hole pulls enemies towards it slower.

3.name: Black Hole Chill Chance Per Second
3.description: Black Hole has a chance to chill enemies within its area of effect each second.

5.name: Black Hole Added Fire Damage
5.description: Black Hole’s damage over time penetrates enemy fire resistance.

6.name: Black Hole Ignite Chance Per Second
6.description: Black Hole has a chance to ignite enemies within its area of effect each second based on your uncapped fire resistance.

7.name: Black Hole Increased Fire Damage
7.description: Black Hole deals more fire damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).

8.name: Black Hole Ward Generate
8.description: Black Hole grants you ward each second.

10.name: Black Hole Increased Damage
10.description: Black Hole deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).

11.name: Black Hole Slower Cast and Increased Damage
11.description: Black Hole deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers), but has reduced cast speed.

14.name: Black Hole Chance To Freeze On Hit
14.description: The explosions created by the {Collapse} and {Pulsar} nodes have an increased chance to freeze enemies.

15.name: Black Hole Blind Enemies
15.description: Black Hole has increased duration and a chance to blind enemies within its area of effect each second.

16.name: Black Hole Recast Chance On End
16.description: Black Hole has a chance to create another Black Hole once it expires.

17.name: Black Hole All Smaller
17.description: Black Hole has a shorter cooldown and costs less mana, but pulls enemies towards it slower and pulls enemies within a lesser area (both multiplicative with other modifiers).

18.name: Black Hole Increased Pull Strength
18.description: Black Hole pulls enemies toward it faster.

19.name: Black Hole Increased Pull Area
19.description: Black Hole pulls enemies within a larger area.

21.name: Black Hole Faster Homing
21.description: Black Hole moves faster toward the nearest enemy.

22.name: Black Hole Hit Damage
22.description: Black Hole’s center hits enemies, dealing spell physical damage.

23.name: Black Hole Prioritises Rares And Bosses
23.description: Black Hole deals more damage to rare enemies and bosses (multiplicative with other modifiers), and it prioritises moving towards rare enemies and bosses.

24.name: Black Hole Added Duration
24.description: Black Hole lasts longer.

25.name: Black Hole Added Duration and Cooldown
25.description: Black Hole lasts longer, but has a longer cooldown.

26.name: Black Hole Extra Black Hole
26.description: A second Black Hole is created 10 meters away in a random direction from the first Black Hole.

27.name: Black Hole Increased Damage At Center
27.description: Black Hole deals more damage to enemies within 1 meter of its center.

28.name: Black Hole One Hole Only
28.description: Black Hole deals significantly more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers), but only 1 Black Hole can be created at a time.

4.name: Black Hole Convert To Fire
4.description:
Black Hole’s base cold damage is converted to fire damage. Consequently this damage scales with increases to fire damage, but not increases to cold damage.

Black Hole lasts much longer, but no longer pulls enemies.

9.name: Black Hole Binary Star
9.description:
Black Hole becomes two stars which orbit each other. One deals fire damage and the other deals cold damage. 

Black Hole deals significantly more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).

12.name: Black Hole Shockwave On End
12.description: Black Hole creates an explosion when it ends that pushes all enemies back, but has a shorter duration (multiplicative with other modifiers). The explosion makes a wave of energy that deals 15 cold damage and has a freeze rate of 30.

13.name: Black Hole Shockwave Per Second
13.description: Black Hole will {Collapse} each second while it exists.

20.name: Black Hole Homing
20.description: Black Hole moves toward the nearest enemy that is not within its damaging area.

29.name: Black Hole Armageddon
29.description: Black Hole casts {Meteor} every second, but consumes Meteor's mana cost every second.

2.name: Bone Golem Added Attack Speed
2.description: Your Golem moves and attacks faster.

5.name: Bone Golem Added Fire Protection
5.description: Your Golem has a chance to ignite enemies, it also has additional fire resistance and deals more damage over time (multiplicative with other modifiers).

6.name: Bone Golem Added Fire Damage
6.description: Your Golem deals additional fire damage with spells and melee attacks.

8.name: Bone Golem Fire Area
8.description: Your Golem deals more damage over time (multiplicative with other modifiers), and {Pyre Golems}’ {Infernal Aura} deals damage in a larger area.

9.name: Bone Golem Increased Move Speed
9.description: Your Golem causes all nearby minions to move faster, including themselves. This bonus is doubled for the Golems themselves.

10.name: Bone Golem Poison Buff to Player
10.description: Your Golem has a chance to grant you a stack of {Putrid Coating} when it is hit. Each stack lasts for 8 seconds and grants you a chance to poison enemies.

11.name: Bone Golem Melee Cold Damage And Chill To Attackers
11.description: Your Golem deals additional melee cold damage and it has a chance to chill attackers when hit.

13.name: Bone Golem Health and Armor
13.description: Your Golem has additional health and armor, as well as more health and armor (both multiplicative with other modifiers).

14.name: Bone Golem Melee Attack Damage
14.description: Your Golem deals more damage with melee attacks (multiplicative with other modifers), and has an increased chance to stun with melee attacks.

15.name: Bone Golem Big and Armor
15.description: Your Golem is larger (multiplicative with other modifiers) and generates more threat, which increases the likelihood of enemies targeting it.

16.name: Bone Golem Protection From Corpse
16.description:
Your Golems have increased size and deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) if you have no other living golems when you summon them.

Your Golems have significantly higher armor for the first 15 seconds of their life when summoned near the corpse of another Golem.

17.name: Bone Golem Undead Armor Aura
17.description: Your Golem grants additional armor to nearby minions, including itself.

18.name: Bone Golem Health On Hits
18.description: Your Golem's hits restore health to you and itself. This effect restores additional health per 2 Vitality.

20.name: Bone Golem Increased Stun Chance
20.description: Your Golem deals more spell damage (multiplicative with other modifiers), and has an increased chance to stun.

21.name: Bone Golem Increased Bone Nova Damage
21.description: Your Golem has a higher chance to retaliate with {Bone Shatter} when hit and its Bone Shatter deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).

23.name: Bone Golem Health Regen
23.description: Your Golem has additional health regeneration based on your total amount of health regeneration.

24.name: Bone Golem Armor On Skeleton Death
24.description: Your Golem gains significantly more armor for 8 seconds whenever a skeleton minion dies nearby.

25.name: Bone Golem Betrayal
25.description: If you only control one Golem, it has a much higher chance to retaliate with {Bone Shatter} when damaged by hits from allies.

3.name: Bone Golem Twins
3.description: You summon an additional Golem, but each Golem has reduced maximum health, is smaller, and deals less damage (all multiplicative with other modifiers).

4.name: Bone Golem Bone Golem
4.description: Your Golems deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers). If Bone Golems have been replaced by another type of Golem, you now have an equal chance to summon a Bone Golem.

7.name: Bone Golem Pyre Golem
7.description:
You now summon a {Pyre Golem} instead of a Bone Golem.

The base physical damage of Pyre Golems' melee attacks is converted to fire. Consequently this damage scales with increases to minion fire damage, but not increases to minion physical damage.

Pyre Golems also cast {Infernal Aura}, a spell that deals a burst of fire damage on cast and fire damage over time afterwards.

12.name: Bone Golem Deathchill Golem
12.description:
You now summon a {Deathchill Golem} instead of a Bone Golem. 

The base physical damage of Deathchill Golems' melee attacks is converted to cold. Consequently this damage scales with increases to minion cold damage, but not increases to minion physical damage. Death Chill Golems' melee attacks gain a Freeze Rate equal to twice their base damage.

Deathchill Golem also casts Freezing Grasp, a spell that deals cold damage and has high freeze rate. Bosses cannot be frozen by this.

19.name: Bone Golem Blood Golem
19.description:
You now summon a {Blood Golem} instead of a Bone Golem. 

Blood Golems can also cast {Sanguine Orb}, a spell that deals physical damage to nearby enemies over 2 seconds.

22.name: Bone Golem Bone Nova Piercing
22.description: Your Golem's {Bone Shatter} projectiles pierce (continue through targets they hit) and travel faster, causing them to also travel further.

26.name: Bone Golem Freeze Rate Per Vitality
26.description: Your Golem's {Freezing Grasp} spell now has additional spell damage and base freeze rate per point of Vitality.

28.name: Bone Golem Fire Aura Consumes Skeletons
28.description: {Pyre Golems}’ {Infernal Aura} consumes {Skeletons} and {Skeletal Mages} that it hits to deal more damage over time (multiplicative with other modifiers) per minion it consumed.

2.name: Snap Freeze Cast Speed
2.description: Snap Freeze has increased cast speed.

3.name: Snap Freeze Reduced Duration and Cooldown
3.description: Snap Freeze has a shorter cooldown, but Freeze inflicted by Snap Freeze has reduced duration.

5.name: Snap Freeze Chill Chance
5.description: Snap Freeze has a chance to chill enemies.

6.name: Snap Freeze Chill Duration
6.description: Chill caused by Snap Freeze has increased duration. If you have taken the {Snap Shock} node, this increases Shock duration instead.

8.name: Snap Freeze More Cold Damage
8.description: Snap Freeze hits deal additional spell cold damage.

10.name: Snap Freeze Crit Chance
10.description: Improves Snap Freeze’s base critical strike chance.

11.name: Snap Freeze Crit Multi
11.description: Snap Freeze critical strikes deal more damage.

12.name: Snap Freeze Shock Effectiveness
12.description: Snap Freeze hits deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) per stack of {Shock} on the target.

13.name: Snap Freeze Reduced Cooldown
13.description: Snap Freeze costs less mana and has a shorter cooldown.

14.name: Snap Freeze Frostbite
14.description: Snap Freeze inflicts {Frostbite} on all enemies within its area of effect.

15.name: Snap Freeze Frostbite Effect
15.description: {Frostbite} inflicted by Snap Freeze has additional Cold Penetration.

17.name: Snap Freeze Reduced Mana and Cone Width
17.description: Snap Freeze costs less mana, but has a narrower area of effect.

18.name: Snap Freeze Increased Range
18.description: Snap Freeze’s area of effect extends further away from you.

19.name: Snap Freeze More Freeze Duration
19.description: Freeze inflicted by Snap Freeze has a longer duration, but has a narrower area of effect.

20.name: Snap Freeze Armor
20.description: You have additional armor and cold resistance while Snap Freeze is on cooldown.

21.name: Snap Freeze Increased Freeze Duration
21.description: Freeze inflicted by Snap Freeze last longer.

22.name: Snap Freeze Steal Protection
22.description: You steal cold resistance from enemies hit by Snap Freeze, causing you to gain cold resistance for 4 seconds and the enemies hit to lose it.

23.name: Snap Freeze Second Charge
23.description: You can store an additional charge of Snap Freeze, but Snap Freeze has a longer cooldown.

4.name: Snap Freeze Knockback
4.description: Snap Freeze knocks enemies back.

7.name: Snap Freeze Cold Damage
7.description: Snap Freeze hits and deals spell cold damage and can apply on-hit effects, but costs more mana.

9.name: Snap Freeze Lightning Conversion
9.description:
Snap Freeze no longer freezes and its base cold damage is converted to lightning damage. Consequently this damage scales with increases to lightning damage, but not increases to cold damage.

Adds 4 lighting damage for each second of freeze duration. 

All chill chance is converted to shock chance, and all nodes which rely on targets being frozen or chilled now work on shocked enemies instead.

16.name: Snap Freeze Frozen Ground
16.description: Snap Freeze leaves a patch of {Frozen Ground} for 5 seconds, which chills all enemies who walk on it. Frostbite inflicted by Snap Freeze last longer.

24.name: Snap Freeze Self Freeze
24.description: Snap Freeze also freezes yourself for 2 seconds. You cannot take damage while frozen this way.

5.name: Summon Sabertooth Attack Speed And Flurry Swipes Damage

8.name: Summon Sabertooth Increased Melee Damage

2.name: Focus Health Regen
2.description:
Directly casting Smite consumes 10 mana to cause a {Fissure} underneath the target, dealing fire damage to enemies who stand on it.

{Fissure} lasts 3 seconds, and can be triggered once per second.

3.name: Focus Increased Regen per Missing
3.description: {Fissure} deals damage in a larger area.

5.name: Focus Negative Mana Boost
5.description: {Fissure} instantly kills enemies that are below a health threshold (percentage of their maximum health).

6.name: Focus Instant Mana Gained As Ward
6.description: {Fissures} can be triggered more frequently.

7.name: Focus Increased Mana Regen
7.description: When you directly cast Smite, you appear at Smite's targeted location, dealing physical damage to surrounding enemies. Smite costs more mana, has a cooldown, and counts as a {Traversal} skill.

8.name: Focus Shock Chance
8.description: Smite hits knock enemies back.

9.name: Focus Increased Ailment Duration
9.description: Smite heals in a larger area.

10.name: Focus Ignite Chance
10.description: You deal additional melee fire damage if you have hit an enemy with Smite recently (last 4 seconds).

11.name: Focus Armor
11.description: You have increased attack and cast speed if you have hit an enemy with Smite recently (last 4 seconds).

13.name: Focus Elemental Protection
13.description: When you are out of mana Smite is free, but cannot heal, cleanse, or create {Fissures}.

14.name: Focus Heath Recovery
14.description: Being healed by Smite grants you or your allies increased health and mana regeneration for 4 seconds (does not stack).

16.name: Focus Overcharged Mana
16.description: Smite hits have a chance to ignite enemies.

17.name: Focus All Protections
17.description: Smite hits deal more damage to ignited enemies (multiplicative with other modifiers).

19.name: Focus Ward Retention
19.description: Enemies killed by Smite have a chance to explode, dealing fire damage to nearby enemies.

26.name: Focus Ward Per Second
26.description: Smite critical strikes deal more damage.

22.name: Focus End Channel Ward
22.description: Smite hits also cast a {Lightning Bolt} at 3 nearby enemies, dealing lightning damage. Smite costs more mana.

24.name: Focus Increased Channelling Lightning Damage Frequency
24.description: {Lightning Bolts} created by Smite hit additional enemies.

25.name: Focus Channelling Lightning Damage Max Mana Damage
25.description: Improves Smite's base critical strike chance.

20.name: Focus End Channel Lightning Damage
20.description: Smite hits have a chance to blind enemies and deal more damage to blinded enemies (multiplicative with other modifiers).

27.name: Focus Burst Mana and Ward
27.description: Smite heals allies for more health.

28.name: Focus No Cooldown
28.description: The {Sacrifice} node costs less health.

4.name: Focus Negative Mana Key Node
4.description: {Fissure} deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).

12.name: Focus Chill Chance
12.description: Being healed by a direct cast of Smite has a chance to cleanse all negative ailments, such as ignite and chill, from you and allies.

15.name: Focus Health Recovery Per Int
15.description: When you directly cast smite, you have a chance to double cast. The additional cast does not incur a mana cost and cannot teleport you if you have the Descend node.

18.name: Focus Cast on Allies
18.description:
Smite hits have a chance to apply {Spreading Flames}.

Additionally, Smite deals more fire damage over time (multiplicative with other modifiers).

21.name: Focus End Channel Move Speed
21.description:
Smite's base fire damage is converted to lightning. Consequently this damage scales with increases to lightning damage, but not increases to fire damage.

Ignite chance from all sources is converted to electrify chance for Smite and effects related to ignite now depend on electrify instead.

23.name: Focus Channelling Lightning Damage AoE
23.description: Electrify applied by Smite has increased duration and additional Lightning Penetration

30.name: Focus Teleport
30.description: Smite deals significantly more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers), but Smite consumes a percent of your current health on cast.
